business.headline: Microsoft to cut 4,800 jobs as AI spending continues to riseThe latest round of layoffs comes at a time when major technology companies are spending billions of dollars on AI infrastructure and facing growing pressure to show returns on those investments.

EPF scheme 2026: Can employers refuse to match higher PF contributions?The new EPF Scheme 2026 has clarified the rules on voluntary PF contributions, but what does that mean for salaried employees? Can your employer say no to matching the extra contribution? Have a look.

Vietnam and 5 others climb World Bank's income ladder. Where does India stand?This year, no country moved to a lower category. Instead, six countries improved their position. Jordan, Micronesia, the Philippines, Sri Lanka and Vietnam moved from the lower-middle-income group to the upper-middle-income category.

Sensex ends 521 points higher, Nifty above 24,400; HDFC up 3%The BSE Sensex rose 521.16 points, or 0.67%, to close at 78,285.07, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 159.50 points, or 0.66%, to settle at 24,430.35.

Sebi plans to make it easier to short stocks. Here's what it means for investorsSebi is considering wider access to stock borrowing and lower collateral for short selling. The move is aimed at shifting activity towards cash equities as derivative losses mount for retail investors.

HDFC, RBL, ICICI jump up to 4%: Why are banking stocks rising today?HDFC Bank led a broad rally in banking stocks after reporting strong provisional June quarter business growth. The move lifted expectations for a solid earnings season even as analysts tracked loan mix, CASA pressure and improving macro signals.

How India kept fuel prices in check while the world burned through its walletsWhen the Strait of Hormuz shut down and crude surged past $120 a barrel, petrol prices rose 64% in Pakistan, 21% in Britain and 85% for diesel in the UAE. In Delhi, petrol went up 7.8% Here is how India managed that, and what it cost.
Apple restarts card payments for app store in India: ReportApple had stopped accepting credit and debit card payments in India in 2021 after the RBI introduced its card tokenisation mandate.

You may not be able to sell your electrical products from Oct 1. Here's whyIndia will require BIS certification and the ISI mark for 90 categories of electrical appliances from 1 October 2026. The phased rollout could disrupt manufacturing, imports and sales for businesses that miss approval timelines.

Can't activate your UAN on the EPFO portal? Here's why and what to do nowEPFO has ended UAN activation and direct UAN generation on its portal after the upgrade. The shift to UMANG with Aadhaar-based face authentication changes how PF subscribers access these services.

Made over Rs 1.25 lakh in equity gains? Here's why ITR-1 may not be enough this yearBefore filing your return, it is worth checking your capital gains statement carefully to ensure you choose the correct ITR form and avoid unnecessary notices or delays.

8th Pay Commission: Odisha talks end today. What has happened so far?The Bhubaneswar meetings, held on July 6 and 7, are part of the Commission's nationwide consultation exercise, where it is meeting employee unions, pensioner associations and other stakeholders before preparing its recommendations for the Centre.
Sensex opens 250 points higher, Nifty above 24,300; HDFC Bank up 2%The BSE Sensex was up 0.33% at 78,020.50, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 0.33% to trade at 24,349.95 around 9:30 am.

Sitting on idle gold? Government may soon give you a reason to earn from itThe Centre may soon unveil a revamped Gold Monetisation Scheme that allows jewellers to collect household gold deposits. The proposed shift is aimed at drawing idle gold into the formal system and reducing reliance on imports.

Want to become a crorepati through SIP? Here's how much to invest and for how longBecoming a crorepati is no longer just a dream for the wealthy. With the right SIP strategy and enough time, you could get there too, but how much do you really need to invest each month?

The company powering Nvidia's AI chips wants a $28 billion Wall Street debutSK Hynix is set to launch a $28 billion US listing after a sharp rally driven by AI chip demand. The offering underlines how companies supplying the hardware behind artificial intelligence are drawing huge investor interest.

Maersk places fresh order for 1,000 India-made shipping containersThe announcement came as Union Minister of Ports, Shipping and Waterways, Sarbananda Sonowal, unveiled the first export-import (EXIM) shipping container manufactured in India for the company at the Maersk-CONCOR Inland Container Depot in UP's Dadri.

E20 even powers F1 cars: Auto experts dismiss concerns, back govt amid backlashIndustry experts defended the rollout of E20 fuel, saying extensive testing found no concerns in compatible vehicles. They said the ethanol push is tied to lower emissions, defined standards and reduced oil dependence.

Carlsberg eyes India stock market listing; confidential IPO filing submittedCarlsberg said it has confidentially filed for an IPO of its Indian business. The proposed listing would allow a partial stake sale, subject to approvals and market conditions.

ITR 2026: Do you need to file two income tax returns? Tax department explainsThe new Income-tax Act has changed the way taxpayers talk about return filing, replacing "Assessment Year" with "Tax Year". But does that mean you'll have to file two ITRs? Here's what the tax department says.

entertainment.headline: Viral: Janhvi, Khushi perform sisterly duties during Anshula's sindoor ritual A photo from social media influencer Anshula Kapoor and her husband, writer Rohan Thakkar's wedding shows actors Janhvi and Khushi assisting during the sindoor ritual. The moment, shared by Anshula on Instagram, has since gone viral on social media.

Diljit Dosanjh reacts to fan's Green Card question amid US citizenship buzz Actor-singer Diljit Dosanjh on Monday reacted after a fan asked him about getting a Green Card amid reports of his US citizenship. He also opened up about his latest film Satluj, its surprise ZEE5 release, and the years-long delays faced by it.

Actors Revathy, Padmapriya resign from Malayalam film body amid internal crisis Actors Revathy Asha Kelunni and Padmapriya Janakiraman resigned from the Association of Malayalam Movie Artists (AMMA). Their exit comes as the Malayalam film body faces internal turmoil.

Arjun Kapoor's sister Anshula marries Rohan Thakkar in Mumbai Influencer Anshula Kapoor married longtime boyfriend Rohan Thakkar in an intimate wedding ceremony in Mumbai. The couple tied the knot in the presence of close friends and family.

Anurag Kashyap reviews Mollywood Times, praises director for truth and idealism Anurag Kashyap has praised Mollywood Times on Letterboxd, calling it "a lot of truth and a lot of idealism" while lauding director Abhinav Sunder Nayak's vision.

Jaswant Singh Khalra abducted again: Kunal Kamra's blistering post on Satluj ban Standup comedian Kunal Kamra has questioned CBFC chairperson Prasoon Joshi after Satluj was removed from OTT within 48 hours of its uncut release. His note has reignited the censorship debate around the film formerly titled Punjab '95.

This one Satluj scene has sparked a fierce online backlash A scene from Satluj has triggered sharp reactions online. The row has renewed scrutiny of the film's portrayal, even as its removal from Zee5 remains under focus.

Satluj: Real story of Jaswant Singh Khalra, a 16-year court battle and the murder The Satluj OTT row has renewed attention on the Jaswant Singh Khalra case, which began with his 1995 abduction after he exposed alleged secret cremations. The case led to Supreme Court intervention, a CBI probe and life terms for six Punjab Police officials.

Lock Upp: Kashmera Shah supports 'mami' Ssunita Ahuja with a poweful note Actor Kashmera Shah came out in support of Govinda's wife, influencer Ssunita Ahuja, after she broke down on Lock Upp and said she wanted to leave the show. The actor shared an emotional note urging her to be strong and reminding her that she is not alone.

Satluj X review: Diljit's film tears heart apart; stunned viewers call it hidden gem Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj has left viewers emotional, with many calling the film a "hidden gem" on X. Despite its removal from ZEE5, the film continues to receive praise for its storytelling and performances.

Infuriating: Anurag Basu, Ranvir Shorey and others slam Satluj's removal from OTT Actor Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj was taken off from streaming in India days after its delayed release, prompting reactions from actor Ranvir Shorey, directors Anurag Basu, Sanjay Gupta and Onir. The move has renewed attention on the film's politically sensitive subject and troubled certification journey.

Sikh body slams removal of Satluj, calls it attempt to suppress historical truth Shiromani Gurdwara Parbandhak Committee president Harjinder Singh Dhami condemned the removal of Satluj from ZEE5 in India. He called it an attempt to suppress historical truth and demanded that the restrictions be lifted immediately.

No pro-Khalistan sentiment, shows truth: Khalra's lawyer on Satluj's OTT removal The takedown of Satluj after its brief Zee5 release has drawn sharply different reactions from people linked to the Jaswant Khalra case. While Advocate Navkiran Singh called the move wrong, former IAS officer KBS Sidhu said it may have been done to safeguard public order.

Aditya Dhar, Arjun Rampal praise force of nature Ranveer Singh on 41st birthday Director Aditya Dhar and actor Arjun Rampal marked actor Ranveer Singh's 41st birthday with unseen Dhurandhar pictures and personal notes. Their messages focused on his performance in the film and their shared bond forged on set.

ZEE5 breaks silence on Satluj takedown, urges viewers not to turn to piracy ZEE5 has issued a fresh statement after pulling down Satluj, urging viewers not to support piracy and assuring them it is exploring every possible way to restore the Diljit Dosanjh-starrer. The film, based on the life of human rights activist Jaswant Singh Khalra, had faced years of censorship hurdles before its surprise OTT release.

Arjun Kapoor is happier than anyone else after sister Anshula's kaleera falls on him Actor Arjun Kapoor appeared happier than anyone else after sister Anshula Kapoor's kaleera fell on him during her chooda ceremony. Sharing the video, Anshula joked that she had never seen him so "committed" to getting chosen by a kaleera.

NTK leader seeks Tamil Nadu ban on Jr NTR-Trivikram film over Murugan row Seeman has asked for a ban on Jr NTR and Trivikram Srinivas's upcoming film in Tamil Nadu over its apparent portrayal of Lord Murugan. The demand follows backlash to the announcement line 'Born in the North' and has widened the debate over faith, identity and creative liberty.

Huma Qureshi dodges question on Toxic delay, says focus should be on August release Huma Qureshi brushed aside questions on the repeated postponements of Yash's Toxic and pointed to its new August 26 release date.

Diljit Dosanjh reacts as fan asks why Satluj chose OTT over theatres Actor-singer Diljit Dosanjh has revealed why Satluj skipped a theatrical release and premiered directly on ZEE5, saying the team faced years of hurdles and was not even allowed to talk about the film before its release. The actor also reflected on the delays and thanked fans for supporting the project.

general news.headline: Cristiano Ronaldo bows out as Spain end Portugal's World Cup dreamCristiano Ronaldo leaves the World Cup without the one prize that always eluded him, but never without the admiration of the game he helped define. Portugal's future may finally be ready to move beyond him, yet their greatest footballing story will forever begin with the man who carried the nation for more than two decades.

How heavy rain cut off Mumbai from three sides and turned it into a virtual islandThree days of heavy rain have crippled Mumbai's road and rail links, stranding commuters across key routes. With more downpours forecast, the IMD has issued a Red Alert and tightened emergency measures.

Champat Rai resigns from Ram temple trust, Krishna Mohan is new interim chiefThe Ram Mandir Trust has accepted the resignations of Champat Rai and Anil Mishra after a marathon meeting on the donation embezzlement case. The Trust also broke its silence over the controversy.

Microsoft to cut 4,800 jobs as AI spending continues to riseThe latest round of layoffs comes at a time when major technology companies are spending billions of dollars on AI infrastructure and facing growing pressure to show returns on those investments.

How Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj exposed Punjab's political fault linesDiljit Dosanjh-starrer Satluj came and went. The competing political readings remain, revealing far more about Punjab's layered discourse than the movie itself.

Saved World Cup from big stain: Trump says he sought review of US player's red cardFIFA World Cup 2026: US President Donald Trump confirmed he asked FIFA president Gianni Infantino to review Folarin Balogun's red card, insisting the decision was unfair and not a sending-off offence. He later welcomed FIFA's reversal, which made the USA striker available for the World Cup Round of 16 against Belgium.

USA 0-1 Belgium Live: Charles de Ketelaare gives early leadUSA vs Belgium Live Score, FIFA World Cup Round of 16: Belgium take a deserved lead as Charles De Ketelaere taps home from close range after the USA fail to clear a deflected cross. The hosts have been under pressure since kickoff and are made to pay.

Video: F-16, Sukhoi-30 jets welcome PM's aircraft in Indonesian airspacePrime Minister Narendra Modi's aircraft was escorted by an F-16 and a Sukhoi-30 as it entered Indonesian airspace before landing in Jakarta. The ceremonial welcome set the tone for talks with President Prabowo Subianto on deepening India-Indonesia ties.

At Tehran's Azadi Square, Khamenei's ringed hand is new symbol of defianceMillions gathered in Tehran as Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's coffin moved from Grand Mosalla to Azadi Square. A giant ringed-hand monument and delegations from nearly 100 countries underscored the funeral's symbolic and diplomatic weight.

Built to beat traffic, lost to monsoon: Why Mumbai-Pune 'missing link' broke downHeavy monsoon rain triggered a landslide near Tunnel 2 and exposed surface defects on the newly opened Mumbai-Pune Expressway missing link. The disruption has turned the project's first monsoon into a key test of its slope stability, drainage and construction quality.

We want peace: Afghans warn Pakistan against crossing sovereignty red lineAfghans are a fiercely independent people. Speak to anyone on the streets of Kabul and they want peace with Pakistan. But there is also a clear red line. If the conflict spills over, many say they are prepared to defend their country.

E10 should continue or entire vehicle fleet…: Old Niti Aayog report flags concernThe six-year-old roadmap shows that while carmakers' body SIAM backed India's ethanol-blending programme, it also pushed for parallel E10 availability, arguing that existing vehicles would need protection-grade fuel during the transition to higher ethanol blends.

Inside Anshula Kapoor's vibrant bridal look, from layered emeralds to coral lehengaAnshula Kapoor married Rohan Thakkar in an intimate ceremony attended by family and friends. Her coral, gold and emerald bridal palette stood out, but the most beautiful accessory was her smile!

Man dies after bank delays treatment money over KYC, family protests with bodyA family in Jharkhand's Garhwa district carried Ratan Lakra's body into a rural bank, alleging delays in e-KYC and cash withdrawal blocked his treatment. The bank denied withholding the money, and the protest has intensified questions over rural banking access during medical emergencies.

Nurse injected toilet cleaner through husband's drip after murder bid failedAccording to police, Sandhya and Prashant had been facing marital discord, allegedly over her relationship with Anil. Investigators claim the trio conspired to eliminate Prashant, who had recently returned from the Gulf, believing he had become an obstacle to their relationship.

How JD Vance won Trump over to become MAGA's undisputed heir for 2028Donald Trump is increasingly viewing JD Vance as the leading heir to the MAGA movement ahead of 2028 presidential elections. Vance's Iran diplomacy, media blitz and Republican support have strengthened his standing, even as some conservatives remain wary.

Satluj review: When a democracy forgets its dead, cinema remembersRelease Date:Fri Jul 03, 2026Satluj movie review: Diljit Dosanjh's film follows Jaswant Singh Khalra as he traces the disappeared through official records and cremation logs. The film turns that investigation into a stark question about memory, accountability and constitutional rights.

At Khamenei's funeral, did Iran use Quran verses to convey messages to countries?Different Quranic verses were recited as delegations from various countries and non-state entities paid their last respects to Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Tehran. Experts suggest the recitations of the verses at the funeral appeared to be messages.

523 trees fall in Mumbai in a day: Experts blame concrete roads, BMC cites weatherMumbai records 523 tree-fall incidents in just 24 hours, leaving one dead and eight injured. While the BMC blames extreme weather, experts point to deeper structural issues that may be worsening the crisis.

What was a hardcore BJP supporter doing at E20 petrol protest in Delhi?New Delhi's Jantar Mantar became the ground for the first protest against the Centre's decision to make the sale of 20% ethanol-blended (E20) petrol mandatory. Among the protesters were ardent BJP supporters and a person who said he had been linked to the RSS for decades.

2,000-year-old gold rings bearing Indian Brahmi inscription unearthed in ThailandThe rings were discovered alongside human skeletal remains during an ongoing excavation at the newly identified Don Yai Thong archaeological site in Thailand's Phetchaburi province, according to the country's Fine Arts Department.

Former UK minister says ex-colonies owe Britain for empire's development workFormer UK Home Secretary Suella Braverman, whose parents are of Indian origin, has stirred controversy by saying that countries once colonised by Britain should repay the United Kingdom for the investments made during the British Empire. Her remarks came as Jamaica prepares to petition King Charles III for reparations over Britain's role in slavery.

Bengal rape-murder: How CCTV footage of girl's final hours led locals to suspectA CCTV clue led residents to a suspect after a 12-year-old girl went missing in Baruipur, near Kolkata. The suspect's alleged disclosure led to the recovery of her body from a pond, sparking widespread protests, a mob lynching and a political face-off.

Meet Krishna Mohan, former IFS officer leading Ram Mandir Trust for nowKrishna Mohan has been appointed interim General Secretary of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust after Champat Rai resigned. The retired IFS officer from Hardoi is also the Trust's newest member.

sports.headline: Mbappe slams Paraguayan senator's racist attack over World Cup defeat Kylian Mbappe condemned Paraguayan senator Celeste Amarilla after her racist social media post following France's win over Paraguay. France's football authorities and Paraguay's government rejected the remarks, with the FFF preparing legal action.

Ronaldo leaves Portugal future undecided, confirms he played his final World Cup Cristiano Ronaldo confirmed Spain's dramatic Round of 16 defeat marked his final FIFA World Cup but stopped short of announcing his retirement from Portugal duty. The 41-year-old said he would take time to reflect before deciding on his international future.

Roger Federer steals Wimbledon spotlight by staying in empty Royal Box Roger Federer became an unlikely Wimbledon talking point after staying in the Royal Box long after most guests had left. The eight-time champion won admiration simply for watching every minute of Centre Court action.

USA vs Belgium Live: Belgium start strong in 1st half USA vs Belgium Live Score, FIFA World Cup Round of 16: Kickoff in Seattle! The Round of 16 clash is underway, with both sides looking to settle quickly. The USA have the backing of a vocal home crowd, while Belgium aim to control possession from the outset.

Roberto Martinez steps down as Portugal boss after World Cup exit to Spain FIFA World Cup 2026: Roberto Martinez stepped down as Portugal head coach after the 1-0 Round of 16 defeat to Spain. The exit ended his contract and may also mark Cristiano Ronaldo's final international appearance.

Cristiano Ronaldo bows out as Spain end Portugal's World Cup dream Cristiano Ronaldo leaves the World Cup without the one prize that always eluded him, but never without the admiration of the game he helped define. Portugal's future may finally be ready to move beyond him, yet their greatest footballing story will forever begin with the man who carried the nation for more than two decades.

Ronaldo holds back tears as his last World Cup ends in heartbreak: Video Cristiano Ronaldo's World Cup dream ended in heartbreak as Portugal crashed out to Spain in the Round of 16. The veteran forward fought back tears after what is expected to be his final World Cup, with football's biggest prize set to remain beyond his reach.

POR 0-1 ESP Highlights: Spain send Ronaldo, Portugal packing in R16 Portugal (POR) vs Spain (ESP) Highlights, FIFA World Cup: (POR 0-1 ESP): Spain are through to the World Cup quarter-finals after Mikel Merino's late winner. Cristiano Ronaldo's World Cup career ends as Portugal fall in Dallas.

Quarterfinal? Will watch Portugal vs Spain with dad: Cobolli after De Minaur win Wimbledon 2026: Flavio Cobolli beat Alex de Minaur in straight sets to reach another Wimbledon quarter-final. The win sustained his sharp Grand Slam rise, even as brutal heat disrupted play and tested everyone on Court One.

Such things happen: Batting coach Kotak defends T20 Champions' woes under Shreyas Iyer IND vs ENG: Sitanshu Kotak defended India's patchy T20 run under Shreyas Iyer as part of a transition phase. He said youth investment, all-rounder depth and patience matter more than short-term noise now.

Saved World Cup from big stain: Trump says he sought review of US player's red card FIFA World Cup 2026: US President Donald Trump confirmed he asked FIFA president Gianni Infantino to review Folarin Balogun's red card, insisting the decision was unfair and not a sending-off offence. He later welcomed FIFA's reversal, which made the USA striker available for the World Cup Round of 16 against Belgium.

Sooryavanshi in, Samson out. Rinku back as BCCI announces squad for Zimbabwe T20IsIndia tour of Zimbabwe: Shreyas Iyer will lead a new-look squad for the three-match T20I series in Zimbabwe, starting July 23. While Rinku Singh is back, India have left out Sanju Samson.

Here We Go: Tottenham land Tonali, take summer spend past 300 million dollarsTottenham have signed Sandro Tonali from Newcastle United for a club-record fee of 100 million pounds. The deal pushes Spurs beyond 300 million dollars in summer spending and confirms the scale of Roberto De Zerbi's fast-track rebuild.

Ronaldo's Last Dance? Portugal vs Spain Round of 16 live streaming, match guideIberian rivals Spain and Portugal battle at Dallas Stadium for a place in the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals. Cristiano Ronaldo faces La Roja's impenetrable, flawless defence as he leads the Selecao into an absolute blockbuster of a knockout tie.

This is our game, not theirs: Klopp hits out at Trump, Infantino in Balogun ban rowFIFA World Cup 2026: Jurgen Klopp condemned reports that Donald Trump influenced Gianni Infantino to overturn Folarin Balogun's World Cup suspension. The episode deepened doubts over FIFA's independence, with Belgium astonished and Klopp warning football's rules cannot bend for power.

Day 8 LIVE: Play suspended with Zverev ahead, Fery beats DimitrovWimbledon 2026 Live score Day 8: Alexander Zverev's fourth-round clash was suspended overnight by Wimbledon's curfew with the German leading 6-4, 7-5, 3-3. Elsewhere, Arthur Fery forced a deciding set with a stirring comeback, Jasmine Paolini ended Alexandra Eala's run, and Flavio Cobolli stunned Alex de Minaur to reach the quarter-finals.

Watch: Harry Kane gets his voice back after viral World Cup interviewHarry Kane laughed off the viral reaction after regaining his voice following England's win over Mexico. The squeaky interview became a hit, capping chaotic celebrations in Mexico City on Sunday evening.

India's Praggnanandhaa finishes joint third as Alireza wins Croatia Rapid & Blitz titleGrand Chess Tour: Praggnanandhaa closed with a mixed blitz day in Croatia, beating Alireza Firouzja but finishing joint third. The result kept him in the Grand Chess Tour race, though St Louis leg now carries real weight.

Delhi HC sets two-week deadline for WFI to rule on Vinesh Phogat show-cause noticeThe Delhi High Court has ordered the WFI to decide Vinesh Phogat's show-cause notice within two weeks after giving her a personal hearing. The case keeps alive a larger dispute over exclusionary selection rules, maternity return, and governance in Indian wrestling.

Crossed red line: UEFA blasts FIFA's U-turn after Trump's red card interventionFIFA World Cup 2026: European football's governing body has blasted the decision to lift United States striker Folarin Balogun's mandatory suspension for the Belgium clash, warning that political intervention directly threatens the integrity of the game.

World Cup: Ex-FIFA chief slams Trump-linked red card reversal for US strikerFormer FIFA president Sepp Blatter has slammed FIFA after the governing body controversially suspended Folarin Balogun's automatic one-match ban, allowing the United States striker to face Belgium in Monday's World Cup Round of 16 clash.

Tearful Casemiro comforted by journalists after Brazil's painful World Cup exitCasemiro struggled to hold back tears as he reflected on Brazil's heartbreaking exit from the FIFA World Cup after a stunning 2-1 defeat to the Norwegian national football team in the Round of 16 on Sunday.

Mexico boss Aguirre steps down after World Cup exit, backs Marquez as successorJavier Aguirre's third spell as Mexico coach ended on Sunday after the co-hosts suffered a 3-2 defeat to England in the World Cup Round of 16, with the veteran manager backing assistant Rafa Marquez to succeed him.

My wife is Argentine: Cristiano Ronaldo reveals funny chat with flight attendantCristiano Ronaldo recalled a funny exchange with an Argentine flight attendant before Portugal's Spain clash. The anecdote briefly eased a press conference dominated by questions over age, form and retirement.

Freak celebration injury! Jordan Henderson rushed to hospital after England winFIFA World Cup: England midfielder Jordan Henderson was rushed to hospital after suffering a serious wrist injury during the post-match celebrations following his side's dramatic 3-2 victory over Mexico in the Round of 16 on Sunday.

Dear Neymar, thank you for teaching a generation what Joga Bonito looked likeFor millions who grew up in yellow and green, Neymar was never just Brazil's greatest player of a generation. He was our childhood, our impossible hope and our reason to believe that football could still be beautiful, even when it broke our hearts.

A red card, 3 phone calls from Trump and a shocking FIFA U-turnFIFA found itself at the centre of a World Cup storm on Sunday after suspending Folarin Balogun's automatic red-card ban, allowing the United States striker to play against Belgium. The decision followed reported intervention from US President Donald Trump, who personally urged FIFA president Gianni Infantino to review the case.

I've lost my voice: Harry Kane revels after England oust Mexico in Azteca epicHarry Kane could barely speak after England's thrilling 3-2 win over Mexico in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16, admitting he had "lost his voice" after celebrating a famous victory with teammates and travelling supporters at the Azteca Stadium.

Watch: Ochoa retires in tears as England end Mexico's World Cup dreamGuillermo Ochoa's storied career ended in heartbreak on Sunday as the legendary goalkeeper retired after Mexico's 3-2 defeat to England in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16.

England survive the fires of Azteca to end Mexico's World Cup dreamEngland survived a dramatic Azteca thriller to beat co-hosts Mexico 3-2 and book their place in the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals. Thomas Tuchel's side overcame a hostile atmosphere, controversy and relentless pressure to keep their title dream alive.

Why Bruno Guimaraes took Brazil's penalty over Vini Jr vs Norway? Ancelotti explainsCarlo Ancelotti revealed Bruno Guimaraes was Brazil's third-choice penalty taker against Norway after Neymar and Raphinha were unavailable. The Newcastle midfielder's miss proved costly as Brazil crashed out of the FIFA World Cup.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
